name: SPM Active increased select SPMs in adults with obesity
summary: An uncontrolled clinical trial in adults aged 50 to 65 with obesity found increased select SPMs and SPM-pathway metabolites after 28 to 30 days of SPM Active.
statement: In adults aged 50 to 65 with obesity, 28 to 30 days of SPM Active increased several HEPE and HDHA metabolites, including 3.5-fold higher resolvin E1 and 4.7-fold higher DPA-derived maresin 1, while D-series resolvins and immune-cell abundance did not increase.
population_or_model: Adults aged 50 to 65 years with obesity in a single-arm bioavailability trial
quantitative_note: The source reports RvE1 increased 3.5-fold and DPA-derived MaR1 increased 4.7-fold after intervention.
caveats: The study was nonrandomized and uncontrolled.
The main support is target engagement, not tissue repair.
D-series resolvins did not increase and immune-cell abundance was unchanged.
